{{Quote|Your predecessor once made the mistake of questioning my orders, Admiral. Surely you are not so foolish as to make the same mistake?|Darth Malak, to Saul Karath|<ref name="KotOR game" />}}
An '''individual''' served as an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan's]] [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]<ref name="KotOR game" /> during the [[Jedi Civil War]],<ref name="KotOR CG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> a [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] conflict between the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Empire and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. The admiral served as [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the entire Sith [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. At some point during the war, the admiral questioned the orders from Revan's [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], [[Darth Malak]], prompting the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] to remove the admiral from the position in the [[Sith armada (Jedi Civil War)|Sith armada]].<ref name="KotOR game" /> Some time after the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3958 BBY]],<ref name="KotOR CG" /> Revan installed the former Republic admiral [[Saul Karath]], who had defected to the Sith Empire from the Republic, as the commander of the entire fleet.<ref name="NEC: TSSW">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' {{C|''The Second Sith War''}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Karath's predecessor was mentioned<ref name="KotOR game" /> in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', which was developed by [[BioWare]].<ref name="Game">{{WebCite|url=http://www.ign.com/games/star-wars-knights-of-the-old-republic/pc-16512|text=Star Wars Knights of the Old Republic|work=[http://www.ign.com IGN]|archivedate=20161027055618}}</ref>
